methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $343M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Velaura AI is a Series C-stage semiconductor company founded by Rajiv Khemani that has raised $343M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors at similar or adjacent stages—including d-Matrix ($2.0B Series C), Tenstorrent ($2.7B Series D), SambaNova Systems ($5.0B Series D), Etched ($5.0B Series B), and Positron AI ($1.0B Series B)—alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $343M capital base, implying a $583M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range from $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.3x across the three public names) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $496M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.5B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Velaura's current operating scale relative to the peer set. Stage-progression yields a floor, while the peer cohort—particularly d-Matrix at the same stage—anchors the mid-to-high range. Revenue visibility and customer concentration are critical unknowns shaping position within this band.
